demographic_audit_note: Audited Atlantis demographics against facility face sheet; corrected insurance from Medicare Straight to Aetna Medicare Advantage and updated the primary phone number to the daughter's (POA) mobile.
stage_1_intake_responses: Q1: Yes, daughter saw a flyer. Q2: To help with the transition home. Q3: Yes, SW mentioned nurse visits. Q4: Patient is extremely anxious about managing chemo-related nausea and IV site care without facility staff — escalated to SW for clinical reassurance.
weekly_facility_update: Week 2: Patient completed final round of inpatient rehab; PT reports patient is ambulatory with a rolling walker. SW targeting discharge for Tuesday, 11/14.
v_card_and_flyer_status: V-Card inserted into daughter's phone contact list; physical program flyer placed in the patient's 'Home Transition' folder.
hha_confirmation_status: In Place (Delayed). Initially 'Not In Place' due to Missing HHA Orders. After SW re-sent the physician's orders, Sunnyside HHA confirmed SOC for 11/14, assigned RN Miller, and set a 10AM-2PM window.
stage_3_followup_audit: Call 1 (SW): Confirmed 11/14 D/C is firm; flagged that Sunnyside was missing the medication titration orders. Call 2 (Family): Confirmed daughter understands RN Miller will arrive Tuesday afternoon and that oxygen equipment should be delivered by 9AM.
pre_dc_pulse_call_result: Reached daughter 24 hours prior; she confirmed the home is ready and the patient is excited but still nervous. Reassured her about the MA visit timing.
atlantis_final_sync: Entered D/C date 11/14 and first MA visit date 11/15 into Atlantis record.
ma_visit_booking: MA scheduled for 11/15 at 09:00 AM. Booking was held until 11/13 when Sunnyside HHA finally confirmed the nurse assignment and 'In Place' status.
narrative_summary: In Stage 1, the Connection Intake identified significant patient anxiety regarding post-chemo symptom management, which was escalated to the SW to ensure clinical alignment. Maintenance in Stage 2 focused on educating the daughter on the Day 1 transition plan and inserting the V-Card. Stage 3 was hindered by missing clinical orders at the HHA, which I identified during the Confidence Audit and escalated to the SW for resolution. Once HHA 'In Place' status was verified, I scheduled the MA visit within the 24-hour window, successfully closing the Confidence Loop.

format_1_state_log.event_description: Atlantis Data Lag: Insurance mismatch between Atlantis and Face Sheet
Missing HHA Orders at Handoff: Sunnyside HHA reports no titration orders received
MA Scheduling Postponed: HHA not 'In Place' due to missing orders

format_2_triples.situation: Connection Intake Q4 surfaces anxiety about managing chemo side effects at home.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Escalated concern to the Social Worker to facilitate a clinical discussion between the nurse and patient.

format_2_triples.situation: Sunnyside HHA claims they cannot set an SOC date because the physician's orders are incomplete.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Called HHA intake to identify specific missing forms and relayed the exact document names to the SW.

format_2_triples.situation: Discharge is tomorrow but HHA has not yet assigned a specific nurse.
format_2_triples.action_taken: Withheld MA scheduling and continued daily follow-up with SW until nurse assignment was confirmed.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Wait for the Social Worker to realize the HHA is missing orders during their own routine discharge check.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Respects the SW's primary responsibility for the clinical handoff but risks a delayed discharge or a failed Day 1 transition if the SW is overwhelmed.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Conduct the Confidence Audit call to the HHA to identify the specific missing orders, then provide the SW with a clear, actionable request to re-fax those documents.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: Aligns with the PN goal of building a Confidence Loop by identifying barriers early and providing the SW with the information needed to fix them without overstepping into clinical management.

format_3_rl_scenario.description: Access the patient's facility chart, print the missing chemotherapy titration orders, and fax them directly to Sunnyside HHA to ensure the SOC date isn't pushed.
format_3_rl_scenario.rationale: While this ensures the transition moves forward, it violates the Banned Actions by touching clinical documentation and performing a task strictly reserved for the SW or facility clinical staff.
